prompt: For every pair of elements in S, either the first is related to the second or vice versa
canonical_formula: all s, t: S | s->t in r or t->s in r
sketch: all s, t: S | s->t _ r or t->s _ r
sketch_hint: Replace each _ with the correct set membership operator

prompt: Every element in S is related to itself
canonical_formula: all s: S | s->s in r
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct set membership operator

prompt: If element x in S is related to y, then y is also related to x
canonical_formula: all s, t: S | s->t in r implies t->s in r
sketch: all s, t: S | s->t in r _ t->s in r
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct logical connective

prompt: If element x in S is related to y and y is related to z, then x is also related to z
canonical_formula: all s, t, u: S | s->t in r and t->u in r implies s->u in r
sketch: all s, t, u: S | s->t in r and t->u in r _ s->u in r
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct logical connective

prompt: If element x in S is related to y and y is related to x, then x and y are the same element
canonical_formula: all s, t: S | s->t in r and t->s in r implies s = t
sketch: all s, t: S | s->t in r and t->s in r implies s _ t
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct comparison operator

prompt: No element in S is related to itself
canonical_formula: all s, t: S | s->t in r implies s != t
sketch: all s, t: S | s->t in r implies s _ t
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct comparison operator

prompt: Every element in S is related to at most one element (making r a partial function)
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct multiplicity keyword

prompt: Every element in S is related to exactly one element (making r a total function)
sketch_hint: Replace _ with the correct multiplicity keyword
